pattern: Collect all length-k substrings into a set and check whether the set reaches the required 2^k distinct codes.
hints: There are exactly 2^k binary codes of length k; if the set of all length-k substrings of s has size 2^k then every code is covered.
Slide a window of width k across s, hashing each substring into a set.
Early exit: if len(s) - k + 1 < 2^k the string is too short to contain all codes.

edge_inputs.description: string too short to contain 2^k codes
k=3 — 8 codes needed, just barely covered
long string but missing one code

stress.description: long binary string with k=10, expected to cover all 1024 codes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    # guarantee all 2^10 = 1024 codes by concatenating them then padding
    k = 10
    codes = [format(i, '0' + str(k) + 'b') for i in range(2**k)]
    rng.shuffle(codes)
    s = ''.join(codes)
    # pad to size with random bits
    extra = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(max(0, size - len(s))))
    return [(s + extra)[:size], k]
